{"query":"Andy Burnham vs candidates Labour leadership contest 2026 latest polling MP endorsements","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1311,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.itv.com/news/granada/2026-06-19/who-is-andy-burnham-and-what-led-him-to-return-to-westminster","title":"Who is Makerfield MP and PM in waiting Andy Burnham and what led him to return to Westminster? | ITV News Granada","content":"The Greater Manchester Mayor defeated Reform UK’s Robert Kenyon by 9,231 votes, up from 5,399 in 2024, and Labour’s vote share increased by 9.61%, on 18 June.
After 349 of the party's MPs nominated Burnham it became impossible for any other candidate to gain the 81 endorsements needed to challenge for the Labour leadership.
From there, he joined Gordon Brown's cabinet as Chief Secretary to the Treasury and later Culture Secretary.
It was during his tenure as Culture Secretary, that he kickstarted the road to a second Hillsborough inquiry.
By the time Josh Simons quit his Makerfield seat to give Burnham another opportunity to return, Sir Keir’s authority was so diminished after May’s local and devolved elections that he could not block his would-be challenger from the ballot paper.
After Starmer's resignation cleared the way for Burnham's bid to become the UK's seventh PM in a decade the MP said he would not call a general election.
From Westminster to Washington DC - our political experts are across all the latest key talking points."},{"url":"https://thedispatch.com/newsletter/morning/andy-burnhams-course-for-the-u-k/","title":"Andy Burnham’s Course for the U.K. - The Dispatch","content":"Andy Burnham’s Course for the U.K.
Daniel Sipes is a Dispatch intern from Washington, D.C.
He graduated in 2026 from the University of Chicago with degrees in economics and public policy and a minor in English and creative writing."},{"url":"https://www.itv.com/news/granada/2026-05-19/reform-uk-and-andy-burnham-to-go-head-to-head-in-by-election","title":"Labour and Reform UK choose candidates to fight Makerfield by-election in June | ITV News Granada","content":"Candidates for the Makerfield by-election are being announced in a political race which could lead to a new Prime Minister.
Announcing his party's candidate, Reform UK leader Nigel Farage MP described the contest as a “David versus Goliath battle”.
If he returns to Parliament, Mr Burnham is widely expected to challenge Sir Keir Starmer for the Labour leadership, but the Prime Minister has insisted he will not “walk away” from Downing Street.
"Make no mistake, that means a new path for Britain."
Mr Kenyon, who was born in the constituency and won 31.8% of the vote in 2024, said: "Makerfield has never had a member of Parliament who was actually born in Makerfield.
The news came as Sir Keir Starmer gathered his new-look cabinet for the first time since Wes Streeting resigned as Health Secretary and called on the Prime Minister to quit.
Pollster Luke Tryl, director of More In Common, said Mr Burnham’s name on the ballot paper could be worth a 20-point boost to Labour’s prospects in a seat otherwise "tailor-made" for Reform UK.
Just 4% ranked Wes Streeting as their first choice, with 57% saying he was wrong to resign as health secretary and 15% backing him in a head-to-head contest with the Prime Minister.
In a contest between Mr Burnham and Sir Keir, 59% said they would back the mayor while 37% would support the Prime Minister."}]}
